completedPhaseClaims.summary: Authored grounded business requirements for a bounded connected graph explorer with real topology, expansion, focus, filtering, path explanation, equivalent nonvisual interaction, responsive/accessibility behavior, honest states, security/privacy, observability, migration compatibility, and mvp release-train alignment.

scopeProgress.name: Bounded Projection Cursor Expansion And Path API
Entry Points Deep Links And Restoration

executionHistory.summary: Created analyst-owned spec.md and state.json only. No design, UX, scopes, report, user validation, source, tests, certification, commit, push, or deployment work was performed.

Authored UX-owned graph screen inventory, reusable primitives, desktop/tablet/mobile ASCII wireframes, equivalent Graph/Outline/Table interactions, accessibility and scale safeguards, user flows, and a stable real-stack Playwright scenario matrix. No design, scope, source, test, certification, commit, push, or deployment work was performed.

Created the contract-grade technical design for one authorized bounded graph-query foundation shared by Browse, Graph, Outline, Table, and Path, using the existing PostgreSQL graph. The design defines concept/entity and edge/evidence contracts, query-bound cursors, deterministic Canvas 2D and semantic projections, path limits, saved-view preferences, privacy clearing, activation, observability, migration/rollback, and Playwright canvas-pixel validation. No spec, scope, report, user validation, scenario manifest, source, test, certification, commit, push, build, or deployment change is claimed.

Reconciled SR-006/GRILL-10/GRILL-12: the explorer projects one operator-owned global graph through explicit grants; connected overview requires at least two real nodes and one stored edge; isolated-only data is no-connected-overview; projections are bounded and equivalent across Graph/Outline/Table; scale, privacy, typed errors, and representative accessibility begin in every slice. Preserved SCN-105-001..013 and added 014..016. No source, tests, design, scopes, report, user validation, certification, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

Reconciled the requested SR-010/SR-011/GRILL-10 graph design and SR-012/SR-013 cross-capability design obligations. Bounded overview assembly now uses authorized stored edges and explicitly computes components; connected requires two real nodes plus one stored edge, while isolated-only and unresolved-partial remain honest. Defined deterministic bounded expansion, projection-equivalent first-party Canvas/Outline/Table, real nonblank and privacy-clear pixel checks, a first-enabled-slice query-plan/grant/security/error/a11y gate, implementation-time migration number allocation, source-locking rationale, validate-plane graph.explorer traces/SLOs, and disposable no-interception graph fault profiles. No spec, scopes, report, uservalidation, manifest, config, source, test, certification, status transition, CCManager mutation, spec 079, commit, push, or deployment work is claimed.

Reconciled the planning packet to the 05:46 spec revision that added SCN-105-014/015/016. mtime drift: spec.md/state.json (05:46:37) and design.md (05:46:38) were fresh; scopes, scenario-manifest.json, test-plan.json, _index.md, report.md, and uservalidation.md were stale at 01:27:57. 105 design.md already specified all three new scenarios, the two-node/one-edge connectedness contract, the component analyzer, and Graph/Outline/Table equivalence, so no design edit was made (no 105 design-staleness residual). Added SCN-105-014 (connected real-edge minimum) and SCN-105-015 (isolated-only no-connected-overview) to SCOPE-01 and SCN-105-016 (Graph/Outline/Table equivalence + Canvas-failure safety) to SCOPE-05 across scope.md Gherkin/impl/Test-Plan/DoD, scenario-manifest.json (13->16 scenarios, 52->64 planned), test-plan.json (52->64 scenario rows), _index.md contract, and uservalidation.md journeys. Per-scope DoD test-evidence parity maintained (SCOPE-01 13/13, SCOPE-05 13/13). Dependency on BUG-080-001 preserved; its unresolved upstream design-staleness gap remains a cross-packet risk routed to bubbles.design. artifact-lint exit 0 and traceability-guard exit 0 (0 warnings, 16/16 DoD fidelity) captured this session. No source, authored-test, test-pass, certification, commit, push, or deployment work claimed.
